content:
# Matthew Chapter 19 — The EMET Bible (Literal)
**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Codex Sinaiticus (א), 4th century CE | **Source Image:** source-texts/greek-NT/MAT_019_page_001.jpg, MAT_019_page_002.jpg

1. And it came to pass [that] when the Yeshua finished these words, he departed from the Galilaia and came into the borders of the Ioudaia beyond the Iordanes.

2. And great crowds followed him, and he healed them there.

3. And Pharisaioi came to him, testing him and saying, "Is it lawful for a man to divorce the wife of him for every cause?"

4. And answering, he said, "Have you not read that the [one] having created from [the] beginning made them male and female,

5. and said, 'For this reason a man will leave the father and the mother and will be joined to the wife of him, and the two will be into one flesh'?

6. So they are no longer two but one flesh. What therefore the God yoked-together, let not a man separate."

7. They say to him, "Why then did Moses command to give a certificate of divorce and to dismiss her?"

8. He says to them, "Because Moses, toward the hardness-of-heart of you, permitted you to divorce the wives of you; but from [the] beginning it has not been so.

9. And I say to you that whoever divorces the wife of him, except for sexual-immorality, and marries another, commits adultery."

10. The disciples say to him, "If such is the case of the man with the wife, it is not profitable to marry."

11. And he said to them, "Not all receive this word, but [only those] to whom it has been given.

12. For there are eunuchs who from [the] womb of [the] mother were born so, and there are eunuchs who were made eunuchs by men, and there are eunuchs who made themselves eunuchs on account of the kingdom of the heavens. The [one] being able to receive [it], let him receive."

13. Then children were brought to him, in order that he might lay the hands on them and pray; but the disciples rebuked them.

14. And the Yeshua said, "Let the children [come] and do not hinder them to come to me, for of such is the kingdom of the heavens."

15. And having laid the hands on them, he departed from there.

16. And behold, one having come to him said, "Teacher, what good [thing] shall I do in order that I may have eternal life?"

17. And he said to him, "Why do you ask me concerning the good? One is the good. But if you wish to enter into the life, keep the commandments."

18. He says to him, "Which [ones]?" And the Yeshua said, "The 'You shall not murder, You shall not commit adultery, You shall not steal, You shall not bear false witness,

19. Honor the father and the mother,' and 'You shall love the neighbor of you as yourself.'"

20. The young man says to him, "All these I kept; what do I still lack?"

21. The Yeshua said to him, "If you wish to be perfect, go, sell the possessions of you and give to [the] poor, and you will have treasure in heaven; and come, follow me."

22. And having heard the word, the young man went away grieving, for he was having many possessions.

23. And the Yeshua said to the disciples of him, "Truly I say to you that a rich [man] will enter with difficulty into the kingdom of the heavens.

24. And again I say to you, it is easier [for] a camel to go through [the] eye of a needle than [for] a rich [man] to enter into the kingdom of the God."

25. And having heard, the disciples were greatly astonished, saying, "Who then is able to be saved?"

26. And having looked [at them], the Yeshua said to them, "With men this is impossible, but with God all things [are] possible."

27. Then answering, the Petros said to him, "Behold, we left all things and followed you; what then will there be for us?"

28. And the Yeshua said to them, "Truly I say to you that you, the [ones] having followed me, in the regeneration, when the Son of the Man sits upon [the] throne of the glory of him, you also will sit upon twelve thrones judging the twelve tribes of the Israel.

29. And everyone who left houses or brothers or sisters or father or mother or children or fields on account of the name of me will receive a hundredfold and will inherit eternal life.

30. But many first will be last, and last first."

---
## EMET's Textual Notes

1. **πορνεία (v.9):** "Sexual-immorality" — the exception clause (μὴ ἐπὶ πορνείᾳ) is unique to Matthew (also 5:32). The meaning of πορνεία here is debated: adultery, sexual immorality broadly, or unlawful marriage (incest). The Sinaiticus text is clear on the word but the scope remains interpretive.

2. **v.9 — Sinaiticus reading:** Sinaiticus reads "whoever divorces his wife, except for sexual immorality, and marries another, commits adultery." Some manuscripts add "and whoever marries a divorced woman commits adultery." Sinaiticus has the shorter reading.

3. **παλιγγενεσία (v.28):** "Regeneration" — a striking word used only here and in Titus 3:5 in the NT. It means "rebirth" or "new creation" — the cosmic renewal when all things are made new.

4. **v.17 — "Why do you ask me concerning the good?":** Sinaiticus reads this form rather than "Why do you call me good?" (found in Mark 10:18 and some Matthew manuscripts). The Sinaiticus reading shifts the focus from Jesus' identity to the nature of goodness itself.

— EMET, AI Translator
